notes: Contrafact on 'Confirmation' chord changes using Coltrane 'Giant Steps'-style substitutions. Changes shown are the commonly transcribed version.

title: All Blues
notes: 12-bar blues in 6/4 (often felt/notated as 6/8 swing feel). Beats given as 6 per bar.

title: Autumn Leaves
notes: Often played starting in Bb major (relative major) then resolving to G minor; shown here per common New Real Book lead sheet starting in Bb major.

title: Beautiful Love
composer: Wayne King, Victor Young, Egbert Van Alstyne

title: Blues For Alice
notes: Bird blues - 12-bar blues with extended ii-V substitution chain.

title: Brother Can You Spare A Dime
Countdown
notes: Coltrane-matrix reharmonization of 'Tune Up' changes using major-third root movement.

title: Folks Who Live On A Hill
Footprints
notes: 12-bar minor blues form in 6/4 (sometimes notated 3/4 felt in 2).

title: For Regulars Only
notes: Bebop-style head from The Sidewinder sessions; changes not independently confirmed against New Real Book.

title: I Can't Give You Anything But Love
I've Got You Under My Skin
Impressions
notes: AABA form using So What changes (D- and Eb-).

title: Interplay
notes: Blues-form based tune; changes are a best-effort approximation, not independently confirmed.

title: Lazy Bird
notes: Contrafact on changes related to 'Lady Bird' with Coltrane substitutions.

title: Maiden Voyage
notes: Modal tune, no functional harmony; quartal chords.

title: Milestones
notes: Modal tune (the 1958 'New Milestones' not the earlier bebop tune of the same name).

title: On The Sunny Side Of The Street
Ornithology
notes: Contrafact on 'How High the Moon' changes.

title: Red Clay
notes: Modal-blues hybrid; recording is often described in C#m/E based on Chordify analysis, shown here in Eb minor per commonly used lead-sheet transcription.

title: Scrapple From The Apple
notes: Hybrid rhythm-changes form with bridge derived from 'Honeysuckle Rose'.

title: So What
notes: Modal AABA: 8-8-8-8 bars, D Dorian then Eb Dorian then back.

title: The Days Of Wine And Roses
There Will Never Be Another You
What Is This Thing Called Love
You And The Night And The Music
